When asked about Iran's participation at the World Cup by Politico last week, US President Trump replied: 'I really don't care.'

Sports.title: Bayer Leverkusen poke fun at Arsenal ahead of Champions League tie with cheeky set-piece post - before scoring from one themselves!
Sports.content:
Bayer Leverkusen poke fun at Arsenal ahead of Champions League tie with cheeky set-piece post - before scoring from one themselves!

Bayer Leverkusen are preparing for a set-piece onslaught from visitors Arsenal ahead of their Champions League clash on Wednesday night, if their latest post on social media is anything to go by.

The Germans host the high-flying Premier League leaders this evening in their Round of 16 fixture with Arsenal the bookies' favourites having strolled through the league phase earlier this season.

Mikel Arteta's side have proved themselves to be ruthless competitors in both penalty areas, and have seen off countless opponents with their prowess attacking corners and free kicks.

And ahead of their meeting, Leverkusen jokingly shared an image on their X account with a sign by the corner flag reading: 'No corners allowed!!!'

'Worth a try', was the caption accompanying the post.

But it was Leverkusen who found the net from a set-piece as Robert Andrich headed home from a corner early in the second half to put the hosts ahead.

Bayer Leverkusen have jokingly claimed no corners will be allowed during tonight's Champions League game against Arsenal

The Gunners have proven adept at capitalising on set-piece opportunities this season

The Germans had earlier poked fun at Arteta's bizarre antics on Amazon Prime series All or Nothing in 2022, in which he attempted to inspire his team by showing them a shining lightbulb as an example of how 'connected' they should be.

The Leverkusen social media admin shared a video in which he proceeded to tape over every light switch in the away dressing room in a desperate attempt to unsettle the Arsenal boss.

The video has been viewed over four million times at the time of publication.

Arteta was fully focused on the challenge that faces the Gunners on Wednesday night, with huge excitement building in the fanbase as the team inch closer to a first league title since 2003-04.

'The only thing you can take is game by game and try tomorrow to be better than the opposition and earn the right to win,' he said.

'We always talk about that. Obviously we play so many games in different competitions - in the next three games we're going to play in three different competitions, against completely different opponents.



'You have to adapt to that. You have to, very early in the match, understand what the game is going to require, adapt to it and be better than them.'

Sports.title: Luke Littler lands £3,200 win on the second day of Cheltenham Festival - after darts rival Luke Humphries picked up bigger victory in first race
Sports.content:
Luke Littler lands £3,200 win on the second day of Cheltenham Festival - after darts rival Luke Humphries picked up bigger victory in first race

Luke Littler bagged himself £3,200 after placing a winning bet on the second day of the Cheltenham Festival.

Just two months after earning £1million from winning the World Darts Championship, he landed a neat sum after putting his money behind King Rasko Grey in the Turners Novices' Hurdle.

Littler, 19, placed a £250 bet, so got £2,500 back in win returns and an additional £700 in place returns.

A fellow punter had put £5,000 on King Rasko Grey and so was left celebrating an astonishing £64,000 triumph.

Littler has been enjoying his time at Cheltenham alongside his beautician girlfriend Faith Millar.

Meanwhile, fellow darts star Luke Humphries hit the bullseye on day one by landing £3,250 on the very first race of the Festival.

Luke Littler celebrated a £3,200 win on the second day of Cheltenham Festival

Littler pictured at the course on Wednesday for Ladies' Day, alongside Millar

Luke Humphries with Nathan Aspinall (left), Littler and Littler's girlfriend Faith Millar

Humphries proudly displays his winning bet slip, with his friend landing an even bigger win

The 2024 world champion was in attendance with darts rivals Littler and Nathan Aspinall on Tuesday and wasted no time in bashing the bookies, placing £1,000 on Old Park Star at odds of 9/4.

And Nicky Henderson’s unbeaten hurdler obliged under jockey Nico de Boinville, coming through in the final stretch and passing Sober Glory, who had led for most of the race.

Humphries posed for a picture with racing analyst Pro Sports Advice, who had landed an even bigger prize of £17,500 after backing Old Park Star to the tune of £5,000.

Humphries, 31, confirmed his winnings would be donated to charity.

Littler, who has usurped Humphries as world No1 by landing the last two World Championship titles, was joined by his girlfriend Millar and was also pictured at the course early on day two.

Littler and Humphries will both be heading 100 miles north to Nottingham for night six of the Premier League on Thursday, where they could meet in the semi-finals.

The friends and rivals have met in the final in both of the last two seasons - winning once each - but have both had a slow start to this year's tournament.



Humphries has yet to reach a final and is currently outside the top four - who qualify for May's finals night at the O2 Arena in London. Littler sits third, having broken his duck last week in Cardiff - landing the week five trophy by seeing off Jonny Clayton.

Sports.title: Tottenham cult hero rips into Igor Tudor - labelling him one of 'the WORST coaches around' - and insists whoever appointed him must get the sack: 'The club doesn't deserve this s***'
Sports.content:
Tottenham cult hero, Mido, has torn into under-pressure Spurs boss Igor Tudor and labelled him 'one of the worst coaches around'.

Tudor was brought in to steady the ship after Thomas Frank's sacking but the appointment has gone disastrously wrong.

Spurs have lost all four games under the interim boss and have been dragged into a relegation scrap. Tuesday's 5-2 hammering at Atletico Madrid has all-but eliminated them from the Champions League.

Tudor's position is under massive scrutiny, with many feeling the club should make a change now.

Mido, who played for Tottenham between 2005-07, scoring 14 goals and becoming a cult hero among fans, gave a scathing verdict.

On X, he wrote: 'Whoever appointed #Tudor must get the sack cause I’m sure he never watched #Juventus under him!! He is one of the worst coaches around!

Igor Tudor is under massive pressure and his short tenure has been a disaster

'No tactics no Game plan and man to man management 0!! Can’t handle the pressure and can’t deal with the media!! Plz sack him today not tomorrow!! No time for bull**** anymore.. the club doesn’t deserve this s***!'

Tudor's management was savaged again on Tuesday, after he opted to give Antonin Kinsky his Champions League debut.

The 22-year-old was then brought off by his manager after making two horrendous errors, with Tudor completely blanking him on the touchline.

On TNT Sport, Joe Hart said: 'You have to be treated like a human, just a little bit. I understand the situation.

'This needs getting a serious grip of. Madness happens all the time in football. That was mad, maybe he didn't handle it how he should have.

'I saw the whole bench's hearts drop. They are saying, "don't worry we are here for you". It's the human side of things. If you aren't willing to show it, that's when it starts to divide.'

As Simon Jones earlier reported for the Daily Mail, the inquest into the heavy defeat took place on Wednesday morning.

CEO Vinai Venkatesham arrived at the club's training ground around 8.45am today.

Mido (right) was scathing in his verdict, calling Tudor 'one of the worst coaches around'

Many view Tudor’s position as untenable but Tottenham have little in the way of alternatives, even though former Nottingham Forest and Everton boss Sean Dyche has been backed into 2/1 by some bookmakers to take over, and the club maintain Tudor will take press conference duties on Friday at 1.30pm ahead of the weekend game against Liverpool.

Roberto De Zerbi and Mauricio Pochettino are contenders to take over in the summer. Indeed, talks are understood to have taken place recently with De Zerbi, who is available now after leaving his position with Marseille.

The Italian particularly is a leading candidate for the summer but efforts could now ensue to try to fast-track that decision.

Sports.title: Cheltenham Festival jockey claims he was 'racially abused' for being Irish by rival
Sports.content:
Cheltenham Festival jockey claims he was 'racially abused' for being Irish by rival

Amateur Declan Queally and de Boinville were involved in a heated exchange before the start of the Turners’ Novices’ Hurdle at Cheltenham on Wednesday

View 2 Images Declan Queally hit out at Nico de Boinville (Image: ITV 2026)

An Irish amateur rider accused top British jockey Nico de Boinvile of making an abusive comment towards him "of a racial nature" at Cheltenham on Wednesday. Declan Queally and de Boinville, stable jockey to Nicky Henderson, became involved in a heated exchange during a chaotic start to the Turners Novices' Hurdle, the opening race on the second day of the Cheltenham Festival.

‌



Tempers flared as the riders jostled for position among the 21 runner field which led to the starter declaring two false starts before the runners were allowed to set off. De Boinville went on to finish second on Act Of Innocence behind the Willie Mullins-trained winner King Rasko Grey while Queally finished fifth on the family-trained I'll Sort That.

‌



Cheltenham races result: Il Etait Temps survives scare to win for England World Cup winner READ MORE :

The war of words continued after the race after Queally told ITV Racing: “The start was a bit of a mess. Being abused by an English rider Nico de Boinville is not very nice. I’m an amateur. I’ve come over here riding in front of my kids and that. Horrific.” De Boinville replied: “Maybe he should look in the mirror.”

‌



At a subsequent stewards' inquiry the starter reported that Jack Kennedy, Queally, Danny Gilligan and Darragh O’Keeffe had not approached the tape at a walk or a jig-jog and thereby caused a false start. Kennedy and Queally were suspended for one day for misconduct at the Start. Gilligan and O’Keefe were suspended for two days as it was their second offence in 12 months.

View 2 Images Nico De Boinville was accused of abuse another jockey (Image: ITV )

‌



The report also said that Queally made a complaint "regarding an incident at the start". Queally told the Racing Post that de Boinville "called me a f*****g p***k a couple of times in front of the ITV camera".

He later told Mirror Racing: "It was of a racial nature. I’ve reported it and it is being dealt with by the BHA stewards." De Boinville was contacted for a response and said he had no comment to make.

The stewards notice said. "The stewards, having heard the initial evidence from Queally and Nico de Boinville, have adjourned the matter to obtain further evidence."

Article continues below

Sports.title: Harry Redknapp lifted lid on relationship with Sir Alex Ferguson after Cheltenham winners
Sports.content:
Harry Redknapp lifted lid on relationship with Sir Alex Ferguson after Cheltenham winners

Former Premier League opponents Harry Redknapp and Sir Alex Ferguson have continued their rivalry in racing as they descend on Cheltenham once more

View 2 Images Harry Redknapp showed there's no animosity between him and Sir Alex Ferguson these days after a special Cheltenham day for the duo (Image: AFP/Getty Images)

Former Tottenham and West Ham boss Harry Redknapp has an extensive history going up against Sir Alex Ferguson in the dugout. However, the retired tactician showed his old adversary nothing but love after each got their first Cheltenham wins.

‌



Manchester United great Ferguson, 84, had a bumper day when two of his horses, Monmiral and Protektorat, won back-to-back races for the Scot. Redknapp, 79, joined in the celebrations when Shakem Up’Arry won the Plate Handicap Chase, and he made sure to include his fellow Premier League legend as he paid tribute.

‌



"To be here now in this position is incredible," he said following his landmark Cheltenham win. "My main worry is that they [the horses] get round safe, I jump every jump with them and think, ‘Please don’t fall or get hurt’. When they come back safe and sound, especially in front, it’s all the better.

‌



"It’s great for Sir Alex to have a winner as well, we had some great days. Going into his office at Old Trafford at quarter past two when the teams were out and we’d watch a bit of racing for 15 minutes and then we’d go out and I’d do my best to try and pull off a miracle and get a result!

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"We both loved the racing and we both loved the football. For him to get two winners today is great and for me to get a winner is fantastic."

‌



Jockey hit with suspension over incident in horse race as stewards issue statement READ MORE :

Monmiral caused a major upset at odds of 25-1 when he took the Pertemps Final for Ferguson's breakthrough win. The Old Trafford icon had owned horses for many years prior to that triumph but had never managed to celebrate a Cheltenham winner.

A great day then got even better as Protektorat topped the podium in the Ryanair Chase. And the Scot could hardly believe his luck as his racing investments started to take flight, with Caldwell Potter adding to his Cheltenham triumphs in 2025.

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

‌



“I’ve finally had a winner here. Two in 40 minutes! It’s unbelievable, isn’t it? It’s a special place," said Ferguson, who is being slated for more success this year. "It has been probably 20 years that I’ve wanted to have a winner here, as when I first started I just had Flat horses and no National Hunt ones.

“I got interested in the jumps with Ged [Mason] and [the late] John [Hales]. It 100 per cent makes it more enjoyable having a winner with friends. Everyone looks forward to Cheltenham, while on the Flat you have the Derby. These two events are unsurpassed.

View 2 Images Redknapp is an avid racing enthusiast, just like Ferguson (Image: AP)

Article continues below

“It’s special here, it’s like the Derby or the FA Cup final and I’ve waited a while to experience this. A lot of people who buy horses have never had a winner, never mind here. It’s a feeling of elation.

“Of course it’s not the same as winning at football, that was my life, I was immersed in that, this is what I do for pleasure so it’s a different feeling. I don’t have to worry about it, I leave that to the trainer.”

Sports.title: Cheltenham tips: Thursday's best bets for EVERY race from our top tipster Newsboy
Sports.content:
Cheltenham tips: Thursday's best bets for EVERY race from our top tipster Newsboy

Newsboy's race by race guide includes a 1-2-3 for seven races on day three of the four-day Cheltenham Festival which has the Stayers' Hurdle as its feature

View 7 Images Newsboy has made his picks for the Cheltenham Festival (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

OLDSCHOOL OUTLAW (1.20) beat Bambino Fever when the pair met at Naas in December - and there’s less between the pair than the betting might have us believe. Placed in bumpers in the first three months of 2025 for Co Kildare-based Garry Caldwell, Oldschool Outlaw joined Gordon Elliott after finishing seventh at Aintree in April.

‌



Walk In The Park’s daughter started out for her new stable in a Listed maiden hurdle for mares at Navan in November and scored by six and a half lengths. She then met last year’s Champion Bumper heroine Bambino Fever in a maiden hurdle at Naas and came out on top by half a length, the front two 11 lengths clear of the third.

‌



Cheltenham festival jockey suspended over false starts on first day READ MORE :

That performance caught the eye of JP McManus and Oldschool Outlaw carried the green-and-gold livery when completing her path to today with a nine-length supremacy over Place De La Nation in the Grade 3 Solerina Mares Novice Hurdle at Fairyhouse at the beginning of last month.

‌



There is plenty more where that came from. Hot favourite Bambino Fever, with Festival ‘previous’ on her CV, has to be feared, with Echoing Silence and Charme De Faust the most convincing candidates for the final place on the podium.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 OLDSCHOOL OUTLAW, 2 BAMBINO FEVER, 3 ECHOING SILENCE

View 7 Images Jordans Cross: Newsboy's pick (Image: PA)

‌



JORDANS CROSS (2.00) is unbeaten over fences when staying upright, and another good account is on the cards.

Successful in a point-to-point at Quakerstown in Co Clare in March 2024, the son of Berkshire started out for Anthony Honeyball with a second in a Chepstow bumper nine months later.

Having gone one better in a maiden hurdle at Southwell in December, Jordans Cross ran second under a penalty at Doncaster two days before the turn of the year and was course for 301 days before going chasing this season.

‌



My selection marked his debut in the sphere with a two-and-a-half-length winning margin at Aintree at the end of October and was challenging for the lead when coming down at the final fence behind Kdeux Saint Fray at Cheltenham weeks later.

But he was back in business with a nose call from Go West at Doncaster 12 days before Christmas. And Jordans Cross made it three wins from three completed starts over the larger obstacles with another minimum-margin verdict from Quebecois in the novices’ handicap chase over course and distance on Cheltenham’s Trials Day card in January. Sam Twiston-Davies’ partner is another 6lb higher today, but has more to give and must go well.

In an open, full-field contest, Meetmebythesea looks primed to go well, while Wingmen and Sixmilebridge are other names to throw into the melting pot.

‌



NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 JORDANS CROSS, 2 MEETMEBYTHESEA, 3 WINGMEN

View 7 Images Wodhooh: fancied for the Mares' Hurdle (Image: PA)

WODHOOH (2.40) saw her burden lightened when Lossiemouth went for the Champion Hurdle - and should make no mistake.

‌



Gordon Elliott’s mare has been a winning machine over hurdles - she’s lost just once in 10 starts in the sphere.

A maiden from four attempts on the Flat for Sir Michael Stoute, Wodhooh was promoted to first place when making her obstacles debut at Listowel in September 2023 but needed no further intervention from the stewards to finish her juvenile campaign a perfect 5-5.

Sent into handicaps last season, my selection carried on the good work with a beating of Joyeuse over this course and distance 11 days before Christmas 2024 and returned to the Cotswolds to lift the Martin Pipe Conditional Jockeys’ Handicap Hurdle here last March, thumping Act Of Authority by four and a quarter lengths.

‌



A first defeat at the hands of Lossiemouth in the Grade 1 Aintree Hurdle the following month was no disgrace, but normal service has been resumed this term.

Wodhooh marked her comeback by landing the Grade 2 Ascot Hurdle in November and yet another personal best - a two-and-a-quarter-length margin from Feet Of A Dancer in a Leopardstown Grade 3 over Christmas - gives Jack Kennedy’s mount a class edge.

Last year’s runner-up Jade De Grugy looks destined for the silver medal once more, with Feet Of A Dancer and Take No Chances fighting it out for the bronze.

‌



NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 WODHOOH, 2 JADE DE GRUGY, 3 FEET OF A DANCER

View 7 Images Ma Shantou: Stayer's Hurdle pick (Image: Alan Crowhurst, Getty Images)

MA SHANTOU (3.20) takes on the toughest assignment of his career - but is expected to prove up to the job.

‌



Emma Lavelle handled the career of 2019 Stayers’ Hurdle hero Paisley Park with consummate skill - and in Ma Shantou the trainer has another top grinder on her hands. Successful in two of his five attempts as a novice last season - he scored at Warwick and

Windsor in the run-up to Christmas 2024 - Shantou’s son finished the campaign with a seventh to Jasmin De Vaux in the Albert Bartlett Novices’ Hurdle at the Festival 12 months ago.

But the seven-year-old has made great strides this winter, returning to action with a handicap score under Harry Cobden here in October.

‌



Ma Shantou was below par when favourite for a similar event at Haydock Park four weeks later, but since then it has been gravy all the way.

Staying in the handicap sphere, my selection bounced back to beat Ace Of Spades over this course and distance on New Year’s Day.

And he made light of the move back up in class to land a punt in the Grade 2 Cleeve Hurdle on Cheltenham’s Trials Day fixture at the end of January, storming up the hill to hold Impose Toi by seven lengths.

‌



The evidence of that performance is that Ma Shantou isn’t finished yet, and he’s the choice for a fourth - and most important yet - victory at the home of National Hunt racing.

In a stellar running of the Stayers’ Hurdle, Teahupoo, first and second in 2024 and 2025, reigning champion Bob Olinger and Honesty Policy command respect, along with regular attender Home By The Lee.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 MA SHANTOU, 2 TEAHUPOO, 3 HONESTY POLICY

‌



View 7 Images Fact To File can defend the Ryanair Chase

FACT TO FILE (4.00) is rightly a warm order to keep hold of the crown he captured in bloodless fashion 12 months ago.

Many will perceive a lack of ambition in the move to come back to the Ryanair Chase rather than seek glory in the Gold Cup - but fortune doesn’t always favour the brave.

‌



Fact To File spared his backers an anxious moment when striding away to justify 6-4 favouritism in this race last March, and it should be a similar story for the Willie Mullins trainee this time around.

Although he was well below form behind Marine Nationale back at two miles at Punchestown in April, JP McManus’s nine-year-old lost nothing in defeat but first prize when a neck second to stablemate Gaelic Warrior in the Grade 1 John Durkan Memorial Punchestown Chase on his return.

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

‌



My selection didn’t shine when sixth behind The Jukebox Man in the top-level King George VI Chase at Kempton Park on Boxing Day, but his win in the Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown last time out is right up there with his career highlights.

Never far from the action, Mark Walsh’s mount strode to the front on the home turn and wasn’t for catching, extending his lead to five lengths from Gaelic Warrior by the time he got to the finish line.

An emphatic winner of the Brown Advisory Novices’ Chase here two years ago, Fact To File should rack up a Festival hat-trick.

‌



King George second Banbridge deserves to be high on the list of dangers, followed by Jonbon and last year’s runner-up Heart Wood.

NEWBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 FACT TO FILE, 2 BANBRIDGE, 3 JONBON

View 7 Images Absolutely Doyen: has been a revelation for Paul Nicholls (Image: PA)

‌



ABSOLUTELY DOYEN (4.40) is unbeaten in five starts for Paul Nicholls - and there’s no reason why he shouldn’t show to the fore today.

Doyen’s son failed to find the winner’s enclosure in five outings in point-to-points and three starts over hurdles for Fergal O’Brien.

But the switch to Paul Nicholls has seen a new horse emerge, starting with a victory at odds of 1-7 against three opponents at Stratford in October.

‌



Absolutely Doyen doubled up in a Sedgefield novice hurdle the following month before stepping into the handicap sphere to rack up the hat-trick at Ascot.

The six-year-old kept up the winning run when landing a qualifier for today at Wincanton on Boxing Day, and the nap hand arrived via a three-and-three-quarter-length humbling in a novices’ hurdle at Musselburgh - Absolutely Doyen carried an 8lb penalty - last time.

This test is obviously more demanding, but it will be a surprise if the 14-time champion trainer can’t tease out further progress from this going-places performer.

‌



In a headscratcher of a Pertemps Final, favourite Supremely West, C’Est Different and the first-time-blinkered Melbourne Shamrock are other names to throw into the mix.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 ABSOLUTELY DOYEN, 2 SUPREMELY WEST, 3 MELBOURNE SHAMROCK

View 7 Images Herakles Westwood is Newsboy's choice (Image: Getty Images)

‌



HERAKLES WESTWOOD (5.20) brings plenty of Cheltenham form to the table - and the booking of top amateur Finny Maguire is another plus.

Warren Greatrex saddled the nine-year-old to novice handicap chase wins at Newbury and Windsor last season, but Saddler Maker’s son, seventh in the 2025 National Hunt Chase before a fifth at Punchestown, has been kept to the home of jump racing in three outings since.

Interestingly, Maguire was on board when Herakles Westwood made his return in a handicap chase for amateur riders here in November and ran second to Hung Jury.

‌



My preference lost a shoe but came home well with a fourth to Blaze The Way over this course and distance 13 days before Christmas, but was back to winning form over a furlong shorter here on New Year’s Day.

Sent off at 11-2 in a field of eight, Herakles Westwood had more in hand than the official margin of victory might suggest when holding Katate Dori by half a length.

Under the circumstances, a 2lb nudge up the handicap could have been worse. In a fiendishly difficult close to Wednesday’s fun, Waterford Whispers, Daily Present, Weveallbeencaught and Ask Brewster are just four horses who merit a second look.

Article continues below

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 HERAKLES WESTWOOD, 2 WATERFORD WHISPERS, 3 WEVEALLBEENCAUGHT

Sports.title: Cheltenham jockeys furious as Ruby Walsh calls for immediate intervention - 'It's a joke'
Sports.content:
Cheltenham jockeys furious as Ruby Walsh calls for immediate intervention - 'It's a joke'

Ruby Walsh and several jockeys expressed frustration with race starts at Cheltenham Festival Day 2 on Wednesday, with the racing legend calling for the British Horseracing Authority to step in

View 2 Images Ruby Walsh wants to see a change at Cheltenham (Image: ITV Racing)

Multiple jockeys voiced their dissatisfaction with race starts at the Cheltenham Festival on Wednesday. The second day of the prestigious meeting was marred by complaints from riders about the starters' procedures before each race.

‌



King Rasko Grey, trained by Willie Mullins and ridden by Paul Townsend, triumphed in the opening Novices' Hurdle. Harry Cobden then guided Kitzbuhel to victory in the second race of the day, the Novices' Chase, securing consecutive wins for Mullins to begin proceedings.

‌



Jingko Blue subsequently claimed the Handicap Hurdle before Final Orders prevailed in the Cross Country Chase. Throughout the afternoon, however, there was evident discontent amongst jockeys regarding the starters' instructions at the beginning of races.

‌



ITV were covering the second day of the festival, with presenter Matt Chapman disclosing that he had spoken to several jockeys following their races. Chapman shared comments from four jockeys who had apparently spoken about the race starts at the festival.

Cheltenham horse HMS Seahorse dies as Festival rocked by tragic news READ MORE :

Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

"They let him go when we weren't ready, it's just a joke," Chapman claims Mark Walsh said, who finished 14th aboard Free Spirit in the Novices' Hurdle.

‌



Chapman then added that Jack Kennedy said: "It's too tight at the start. There are too many runners, it's too tight and we're being packed together."

Brian Hughes reportedly had stern words about the race starts on day two. Chapman reported that Hughes told him: "Horrific, horrendous. The outside horses are cantering in, everyone on the inside are jogging in, the ones on the inside basically have no chance."

View 2 Images There have been frustrations with the start line at Cheltenham (Image: Tom Jenkins, Getty Images)

‌



Gavin Sheehan, jockey for Wendigo who fell in the Brown Advisory Novices' Chase, allegedly told Chapman: "We are saying no and the starter says go."

Racing icon Ruby Walsh echoed the sentiments of the jockeys and urged the British Horseracing Authority to intervene.

"The BHA [British Horseracing Authority] need to give their starters the right mechanisms to start races," Walsh told ITV. "A quarter of this field had no chance."

Article continues below

Sports.title: Value Scope: Each way horseracing tips from Steve Jones for Cheltenham Festival Thursday on ITV
Sports.content:
Value Scope: Each way horseracing tips from Steve Jones for Cheltenham Festival Thursday on ITV

The ace tipster has another four recommendations from day three of the four day Cheltenham Festival

View Image Steve Jones has tips for the Cheltenham Festival on ITV

The future is bright for the Ryanair Mares’ Novices Hurdle Willie Mullins won this Grade 2 contest in each of the first five years after it was introduced to the Festival but hasn’t been successful since 2020.

‌



The champion trainer has half a dozen runners this year – including hot favourite Bambino Fever – but I prefer FUTURE PROSPECT (1.20). A tendency to pull too hard in the early stages held her back in four bumpers races but she settled much better when making an impressive hurdles debut on her return to action at Naas in January. Her jumping was good and she seemed much happier being ridden prominently. Have 1pt each-way at 20-1. Plenty of bookies are paying out on four places.

‌



Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

Queen Camilla sports flamboyant hat at Cheltenham's Ladies Day with Carole Middleton READ MORE :

WINGMEN (2.00) looks ready to take off in the Jack Richards Novices’ Handicap Chase. He put it up to smart Final Demand on his chase debut at Navan and he’s twice run sound races since.

‌



Gordon Elliott’s runner was a decent novice hurdler last season. He twice hit the frame in Grade 1 contests and he has a touch of class. A well-run 2m4f on decent ground looks ideal. Have 1pt each-way at 12-1. Most firms are offering five places.

CLASSIC KING (4.40) hasn’t quite hit top form this season but he looks primed for the Pertemps Network Final. He won over hurdles and fences last season before signing off for the summer with an excellent Kempton second behind smart Aston Martini.

‌

Following a promising comeback run when fourth at Cheltenham, he found the ground too soft at Newbury and wasn’t suited by being ridden more prominently at Wincanton on Boxing Day. The demands of this competitive handicap hurdle could be ideal. Have 1pt each-way at 25-1. Sky Bet is going seven places.

The likely strong pace in the Rosconn Group Fulke Walwyn Kim Muir Challenge Cup should suit PRENDS GARDE A TOI (5.20). He’s a strong stayer and he wasn’t helped by an early dawdle when fourth in the Grand National Trial at Punchestown last month.

Article continues below

They usually go a proper pace in this amateurs chase and he’s got a good jockey on board in Barry O’Neill. Have 1pt each-way at 12-1. Plenty of firms are paying five places.

Sports.title: Cheltenham races result: Il Etait Temps survives scare to win for England World Cup winner
Sports.content:
Cheltenham races result: Il Etait Temps survives scare to win for England World Cup winner

Willie Mullins claimed the Queen Mother Champion Chase for the family of Craig Kieswetter, who earned man of the match honours in the 2010 ICC World Twenty20

View 2 Images It Etait Temps wins the Champion Chase under Paul Townend (Image: PA)

Il Etait Temps survived a final fence scare to claim big race honours for the family of an England World Cup winner. The Willie Mullins-trained grey was the clear second choice in the betting for the BetMGM Queen Mother Champion Chase behind JP McManus-owned stablemate Majborough, at 5-6 one of the supposed bankers of the week.

‌



Yet after getting to the front, Majborough made a litany of errors which were to take their toll as he dropped away leaving Paul Townend to make his winning move on Il Etait Temps. The pair led over the second last and quickened away only for a stumble at the last which nearly halted his momentum but they recovered well to pull away again for a ten length win.

‌



Cheltenham horse HMS Seahorse dies as Festival rocked by tragic news READ MORE :

Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

The 50-1 chance Libberty Hunter, a first runner in the name of Cath Williams whose husband Evan gave up his licence after being convicted of assaulting a dog walker with a hockey stick, finished best to fill second place.

‌



Ii Etait Temps is owned by Hollywood Racing and Barnane Stud, which is run by Craig Kieswetter and his family. Kieswetter was man of the match in the England cricket-team which won the ICC World Twenty20 in 2010.

He said: “It’s incredible. We’ve had a few attempts. I’m a bit speechless to be honest. We’ve all flown over from South Africa. You can never question Willie and Jackie and it’s great to be a part of their team.”

View 2 Images Craig Kieswetter: played 71 times for England's white ball team (Image: Getty Images)

‌



It Etait Temps was winded when he took a heavy fall at Ascot in January but recovered to claim his seventh Grade 1 victory.

“I thought they were going to fast and it’s fantastic to have a jockey like Paul that knows they’re going too fast,” said Mullins.

“As they were coming down the hill at the third last I could see Paul getting more confident in the saddle.

Article continues below

“We all got a heart attack at the last but Paul had him in a good position and that bit of confidence he had not to follow the pace was crucial to me in winning the race”

The trainer added: “Majborough can certainly gallop and certainly jump but today it fell apart.”

Sports.title: Man Utd and Tottenham managerial target set for firm 'contract offer in just weeks'
Sports.content:
Man Utd and Tottenham managerial target set for firm 'contract offer in just weeks'

Tottenham and Manchester United will need permanent managerial appointments come the summer, with Igor Tudor and Michael Carrick only signed up respectively until the end of the season

View 3 Images Man United and Spurs are both poised to show interest in Iraola (Image: Getty Images)

Bournemouth are reportedly set to initiate contract discussions with Andoni Iraola during the forthcoming international break amid speculation linking him with Tottenham and Manchester United come the summer. Interim boss Igor Tudor's position at Spurs appears increasingly precarious following a woeful sequence of four consecutive losses.

‌



The club have now suffered six defeats on the spin for the first time in their existence after Tuesday's embarrassing 5-2 capitulation against Atletico Madrid in the Champions League. Sunday's clash at Anfield has become essential as Spurs attempt to distance themselves from the relegation battle.

‌



An appointment of a permanent manager could be imminent, and Iraola, whose existing contract runs out at the season's end, has been considered at Spurs, according to the i paper. Prior to selecting Thomas Frank last summer, Tottenham had Iraola amongst their candidates, and he is guaranteed to be in their considerations once more should they secure survival.

‌



Tottenham make Igor Tudor sack decision with Liverpool confirmation READ MORE :

The report suggests that Bournemouth are anxious to settle Iraola's future before the campaign concludes, acutely conscious of his reputation within the game.

The Cherries' hierarchy reportedly feel they have demonstrated genuine ambition and exhausted every avenue to align with Iraola's objectives, despite a campaign where they've been forced to offload players to meet Profitability and Sustainability Rules (PSR) requirements.

However, it's understood that negotiations over fresh terms started 'over a year ago' and Bournemouth are yet to achieve a breakthrough, suggesting Iraola could be weighing up his alternatives.

‌



View 3 Images Andoni Iraola has done an impressive job at Bournemouth (Image: Getty Images)

For the time being, discussions are reported to be paused, with Iraola concentrating on delivering another top-10 finish.

With the March international break looming, and a three-week interval in the Cherries' fixture list, that timeframe is seen as the perfect opportunity to resume talks.

‌



View 3 Images Michael Carrick is currently in interim charge of Manchester United. (Image: George Wood/Getty Images)

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Bournemouth remain optimistic that, having witnessed what happened to other rising managers such as Frank and Ange Postecoglou, Iraola will conclude that remaining at a steady club with a clearly-defined framework is the sensible choice.

Article continues below

Apart from Spurs, United are also referenced as holding an interest in Iraola should they opt against making interim head coach Michael Carrick permanent.

Iraola is also drawing attention from Crystal Palace, who will lose Oliver Glasner at the season's end.
